0),\n    canvasWidth: Math.round(canvasRect?.width ?? 0),\n    chatPosition: chatPane ? window.getComputedStyle(chatPane).position : null,\n    transcriptOverflowY: transcript ? window.getComputedStyle(transcript).overflowY : null,\n    transcriptScrollable: transcript ? transcript.scrollHeight >= transcript.clientHeight : false,\n    composerDisabled: composer?.disabled ?? null,\n    initialLogEntries: document.querySelectorAll('.workspace-chat-entry').length,\n  };\n});\nawait browser.close();\nconsole.log(JSON.stringify(metrics, null, 2));\nEOF\"","aggregated_output":"{\n  \"chatWidth\": 387,\n  \"canvasWidth\": 773,\n  \"chatPosition\": \"sticky\",\n  \"transcriptOverflowY\": \"auto\",\n  \"transcriptScrollable\": true,\n  \"composerDisabled\": false,\n  \"initialLogEntries\": 2\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.codex.jsonlines:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /Users/kevin/.codex/skills/odysseus-webapp-visual-qa/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: odysseus-webapp-visual-qa\ndescription: Use when working on Odysseus webapp styling, layout, spacing, color, typography, or other visual-polish requests where live viewport screenshots should guide the work. Prefer this skill for most stylistic tweaks, verify changes against the running Docker Compose app, use the dedicated Odysseus screenshot CLI if it exists, and otherwise drive the app with Playwright and capture viewport screenshots.\nmetadata:\n  short-description: Screenshot-backed webapp styling workflow\n---\n\n# Odysseus Webapp Visual QA\n\n## When To Use\n\n- Use for most stylistic tweaks in the Odysseus webapp: colors, spacing, typography, layout balance, pane sizing, header polish, card treatment, menu behavior, and responsive cleanup.\n- Use when the user asks for screenshots, visual validation, UI polish, or requests that are best judged by looking at the rendered app instead of only reading code.\n- Skip this skill for backend-only work unless the request also needs UI verification.\n\n## Core Workflow\n\n1. Check whether the repo has a dedicated Odysseus screenshot or browser-navigation CLI command.\n- Prefer the dedicated `odysseus` command when it exists.\n- Check with `odysseus --help` or inspect `/Users/kevin/code/odysseus/src/odysseus/cli.py`.\n- If the dedicated command is absent, say so briefly in your own reasoning and use the Playwright fallback below.\n\n2. Reuse the Docker Compose app instead of starting a separate server.\n- Check the stack with `docker compose ps --format json`.\n- Treat `web` on `http://127.0.0.1:5173` and `api` on `http://127.0.0.1:8000` as the default local targets.\n- Never spin up a separate Vite or FastAPI server just for visual QA when the Compose stack is available.\n\n3. If the Compose stack is down during Codex code-editing work, start it automatically in a Codex terminal.\n- For Codex code-agent editing flows, do not stop to ask the user first.\n- Start the stack in detached mode with `docker compose up -d`.\n- Mention in an intermediary update that you started the stack because the visual QA workflow depends on the running app.\n- Only pause and surface the issue if the compose start fails.\n\n4. Capture a baseline screenshot before editing when current visuals matter.\n- Default to a desktop viewport of `1440x900`.\n- Use a second narrow/mobile viewport when the change could affect responsiveness.\n- Prefer viewport screenshots of the visible UI, not `fullPage`, unless the user specifically wants the entire scroll area.\n\n5. Navigate with stable selectors, then capture after edits.\n- Prefer role, label, and visible-text selectors over brittle structure selectors.\n- Wait for the relevant pane, menu, or state to settle before capture.\n- Compare before and after screenshots from the same route and viewport.\n\n## Current Fallback When The Dedicated CLI Is Absent\n\nCheck whether Compose is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose ps --format json\n```\n\nStart it automatically for Codex editing runs when needed:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up -d\n```\n\nTake a straight viewport screenshot:\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kevin/code/odysseus/webui && npx playwright screenshot --viewport-size=1440,900 http://127.0.0.1:5173 /tmp/odysseus-visual-qa.png\n```\n\nFor scripted navigation, use a short task-specific Playwright script:\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kevin/code/odysseus/webui && node --input-type=module - <<'EOF'\nimport { chromium } from \"playwright\";\n\nconst browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\nconst context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 900 } });\nconst page = await context.newPage();\n\nawait page.goto(\"http://127.0.0.1:5173\");\nawait page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"New Theme\" }).click();\nawait page.screenshot({ path: \"/tmp/odysseus-visual-qa.png\" });\n\nawait browser.close();\nEOF\n```\n\n## Guidelines\n\n- Use live screenshots to decide whether a styling tweak is actually complete.\n- Mention the exact screenshot path in the final response when you capture one.\n- Prefer `/tmp/odysseus-visual-qa-*.png` for throwaway artifacts unless the user asked for a project artifact.\n- For color, spacing, and hierarchy tweaks, compare before and after in the same viewport.\n- For larger UI restyling, verify at least one desktop viewport and one narrow/mobile viewport.\n- If the app fails to render or the screenshot workflow breaks, say exactly what blocked validation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

\"8568:4443\"\n    volumes:\n      - gcs_data:/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD-SHELL\n        - wget -q -O - http://127.0.0.1:4443/storage/v1/b >/dev/null\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  backend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../backend\n    depends_on:\n      postgres:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      gcs:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: /workspace/secrets.yaml\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    ports:\n      - \"8565:8565\"\n    command: python -m app\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - python\n        - -c\n        - |\n          import sys, urllib.request\n          urllib.request.urlopen(\"http://127.0.0.1:8565/health\")\n          sys.exit(0)\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  worker:\n    build:\n      context: ../../backend\n    depends_on:\n      postgres:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      gcs:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    restart: unless-stopped\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: \"\"\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY: ${STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY:-worker-placeholder-key}\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    command:\n      - python\n      - -m\n      - app.worker\n      - --poll-interval-seconds\n      - \"2\"\n      - --lease-duration-seconds\n      - \"60\"\n\n  frontend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../frontend\n    depends_on:\n      backend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    environment:\n      CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: \"true\"\n      VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565\n    volumes:\n      - ../../frontend:/app\n      - frontend_node_modules:/app/node_modules\n    ports:\n      - \"8566:8566\"\n    command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8566\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - node\n        - -e\n        - |\n          require(\"http\").get(\"http://127.0.0.1:8566\", (response) => {\n            process.exit(response.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1);\n          }).on(\"error\", () => process.exit(1));\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 10s\n\n  browser:\n    build:\n      context: ../../tools/webapp-qa\n    depends_on:\n      frontend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa\n    environment:\n      PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium\n      TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566\n    volumes:\n      - tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs\"","aggregated_output":"import fs from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport path from \"node:path\";\nimport process from \"node:process\";\n\nimport puppeteer from \"puppeteer\";\n\nfunction parseArgs(argv) {\n  const args = {};\n\n  for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {\n    const token = argv[index];\n\n    if (!token.startsWith(\"--\")) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const key = token.slice(2);\n    const value = argv[index + 1];\n    args[key] = value;\n    index += 1;\n  }\n\n  return args;\n}\n\nasync function loadSpec(specPath) {\n  const resolvedPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), specPath);\n  const rawSpec = await fs.readFile(resolvedPath, \"utf8\");\n  const spec = JSON.parse(rawSpec);\n  return {\n    resolvedPath,\n    spec,\n  };\n}\n\nasync function ensureParentDirectory(filePath) {\n  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });\n}\n\nasync function saveFailureScreenshot(\n  page,\n  outputPath = \"../../.artifacts/webapp-qa/failure.png\",\n) {\n  const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n  await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n  await page.screenshot({\n    path: resolvedOutputPath,\n    fullPage: true,\n  });\n  console.error(`Saved failure screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n}\n\nasync function waitForText(page, text, timeout) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    (expectedText) => document.body?.innerText.includes(expectedText),\n    { timeout },\n    text,\n  );\n}\n\nasync function assertText(page, text, timeout) {\n  try {\n    await waitForText(page, text, timeout);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body?.innerText || \"\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `Expected text not found: \"${text}\"\\nCurrent page text:\\n${bodyText}`,\n      { cause: error },\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runStep(page, step, defaultTimeout, fallbackOutputPath) {\n  const timeout = step.timeout ?? defaultTimeout;\n\n  switch (step.action) {\n    case \"goto\":\n      await page.goto(step.url, {\n        waitUntil: step.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForText\":\n      await waitForText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"click\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"type\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector, { clickCount: 3 });\n      if (step.clear ?? true) {\n        await page.keyboard.press(\"Backspace\");\n      }\n      await page.type(step.selector, step.text, {\n        delay: step.delay ?? 20,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"press\":\n      await page.keyboard.press(step.key);\n      return;\n\n    case \"hover\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.hover(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertText\":\n      await assertText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertUrlIncludes\":\n      await page.waitForFunction(\n        (expectedFragment) => window.location.href.includes(expectedFragment),\n        { timeout },\n        step.value,\n      );\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForTimeout\":\n      await new Promise((resolve) => {\n        setTimeout(resolve, step.timeout ?? 500);\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"screenshot\": {\n      const outputPath = path.resolve(\n        process.cwd(),\n        step.outputPath ?? fallbackOutputPath,\n      );\n      await ensureParentDirectory(outputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: outputPath,\n        fullPage: step.fullPage ?? true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${outputPath}`);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    default:\n      throw new Error(`Unsupported step action: ${step.action}`);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));\n\n  if (!args.spec) {\n    throw new Error(\"Usage: npm run check -- --spec <path-to-spec.json>\");\n  }\n\n  const { resolvedPath, spec } = await loadSpec(args.spec);\n  const launchOptions = {\n    args: [\"--no-sandbox\", \"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"],\n    headless: spec.headless ?? true,\n  };\n\n  if (process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH) {\n    launchOptions.executablePath = process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH;\n  }\n\n  if (spec.launchArgs?.length) {\n    launchOptions.args = [...launchOptions.args, ...spec.launchArgs];\n  }\n\n  const browser = await puppeteer.launch(launchOptions);\n\n  try {\n    const page = await browser.newPage();\n    const viewport = spec.viewport ?? { width: 1440, height: 960 };\n    const timeout = spec.timeout ?? 15000;\n    const outputPath = spec.outputPath ?? \"./.artifacts/webapp-qa/latest.png\";\n\n    await page.setViewport(viewport);\n    page.setDefaultTimeout(timeout);\n    page.on(\"console\", (message) => {\n      console.log(`[browser:${message.type()}] ${message.text()}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"pageerror\", (error) => {\n      console.error(`[browser:pageerror] ${error.message}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"requestfailed\", (request) => {\n      console.error(\n        `[browser:requestfailed] ${request.method()} ${request.url()} ${request.failure()?.errorText || \"\"}`,\n      );\n    });\n\n    if (spec.url) {\n      await page.goto(spec.url, {\n        waitUntil: spec.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n    }\n\n    for (const step of spec.steps ?? []) {\n      await runStep(page, step, timeout, outputPath);\n    }\n\n    if (!spec.steps?.some((step) => step.action === \"screenshot\")) {\n      const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n      await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: resolvedOutputPath,\n        fullPage: true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n    }\n\n    console.log(`Completed spec ${resolvedPath}`);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const pages = await browser.pages();\n    const page = pages.at(-1);\n\n    if (page) {\n      await saveFailureScreenshot(page);\n    }\n\n    throw error;\n  } finally {\n    await browser.close();\n  }\n}\n\nmain().catch((error) => {\n  console.error(error instanceof Error ? error.stack : error);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

'application/json', body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Hello from FastAPI\"'!'\"' }) });\n      return;\n    }\n    if (url.includes('/api/v1/sessions/moonlit-harbor')) {\n      request.respond({ page.evaluate(() => {\n    const shell = document.querySelector('[data-testid=\\\"workspace-route\\\"]');\n    const chat = document.querySelector('.workspace-pane--chat');\n    const canvas = document.querySelector('.workspace-pane--canvas');\n    if (\"'!(shell instanceof HTMLElement) || !(chat instanceof HTMLElement) || !(canvas instanceof HTMLElement)) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const shellRect = shell.getBoundingClientRect();\n    const chatRect = chat.getBoundingClientRect();\n    const canvasRect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n\n    return {\n      shellWidth: Math.round(shellRect.width),\n      chatWidth: Math.round(chatRect.width),\n      canvasWidth: Math.round(canvasRect.width),\n      chatRatio: Number((chatRect.width / shellRect.width).toFixed(3)),\n      isStacked: chatRect.top < canvasRect.top && Math.abs(chatRect.left - canvasRect.left) < 4\n    };\n  });\n\n  const path = `${outputDir}/${name}.png`;\n  await page.screenshot({ path, fullPage: true });\n  await page.close();\n  return { path, metrics };\n}\n\nconst desktop = await capture('\"'prompt-22-workspace-desktop', { width: 1440, height: 900 });\nconst mobile = await capture('prompt-22-workspace-mobile', { width: 390, height: 844 });\n\nawait browser.close();\nconsole.log(JSON.stringify({ desktop, mobile }, null, 2));\nEOF\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}

  pitch_index: 0,\n    title: 'Lanterns Over Juniper Lake',\n    logline: 'A child and an otter guardian follow drifting lanterns across the harbor to return each light before bedtime.'\n  },\n  selected_character_sheet: null,\n  selected_story_setup: {\n    id: 'setup-1',\n    revision_number: 1,\n    target_word_count: 1500,\n    target_runtime_minutes: 12,\n    chapter_count: 4,\n    chapter_style: 'short'\n  },\n  active_composition_job: null,\n  active_audio_job: null,\n  latest_story_asset: null,\n  latest_audio_asset: null\n};\n\nconst outputDir = '/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa';\nawait fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true });\n\nconst browser = await puppeteer.launch({\n  executablePath: process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH,\n  headless: true,\n  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox']\n});\n\nasync function capture(name, viewport) {\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.setViewport(viewport);\n  await page.setRequestInterception(true);\n  page.on('request', (request) => {\n    const url = request.url();\n    if (url.endsWith('/api/hello')) {\n      request.respond({ status: 200, contentType: 'application/json', body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Hello from FastAPI\"'!'\"' }) });\n      return;\n    }\n    if (url.includes('/api/v1/sessions/moonlit-harbor')) {\n      request.respond({ status: 200, contentType: 'application/json', body: JSON.stringify(snapshot) });\n      return;\n    }\n    request.continue();\n  });\n\n  await page.goto('http://frontend:8566/sessions/moonlit-harbor', { waitUntil: 'networkidle2' });\n  await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid=\\\"workspace-route\\\"]');\n\n  const metrics = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const shell = document.querySelector('[data-testid=\\\"workspace-route\\\"]');\n    const chat = document.querySelector('.workspace-pane--chat');\n    const canvas = document.querySelector('.workspace-pane--canvas');\n\n    if (\n      shell instanceof HTMLElement &&\n      chat instanceof HTMLElement &&\n      canvas instanceof HTMLElement\n    ) {\n      const shellRect = shell.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const chatRect = chat.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const canvasRect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n\n      return {\n        shellWidth: Math.round(shellRect.width),\n        chatWidth: Math.round(chatRect.width),\n        canvasWidth: Math.round(canvasRect.width),\n        chatRatio: Number((chatRect.width / shellRect.width).toFixed(3)),\n        isStacked:\n          chatRect.top < canvasRect.top &&\n          Math.abs(chatRect.left - canvasRect.left) < 4,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return null;\n  });\n\n  const path = \"'`${outputDir}/${name}.png`;\n  await page.screenshot({ path, fullPage: true });\n  await page.close();\n  return { path, metrics };\n}\n\nconst desktop = await capture('\"'prompt-22-workspace-desktop', {\n  width: 1440,\n  height: 900,\n});\nconst mobile = await capture('prompt-22-workspace-mobile', {\n  width: 390,\n  height: 844,\n});\n\nawait browser.close();\nconsole.log(JSON.stringify({ desktop, mobile }, null, 2));\nEOF\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}

    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /Users/kevin/.codex/skills/odysseus-webapp-visual-qa/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: odysseus-webapp-visual-qa\ndescription: Use when working on Odysseus webapp styling, layout, spacing, color, typography, or other visual-polish requests where live viewport screenshots should guide the work. Prefer this skill for most stylistic tweaks, verify changes against the running Docker Compose app, use the dedicated Odysseus screenshot CLI if it exists, and otherwise drive the app with Playwright and capture viewport screenshots.\nmetadata:\n  short-description: Screenshot-backed webapp styling workflow\n---\n\n# Odysseus Webapp Visual QA\n\n## When To Use\n\n- Use for most stylistic tweaks in the Odysseus webapp: colors, spacing, typography, layout balance, pane sizing, header polish, card treatment, menu behavior, and responsive cleanup.\n- Use when the user asks for screenshots, visual validation, UI polish, or requests that are best judged by looking at the rendered app instead of only reading code.\n- Skip this skill for backend-only work unless the request also needs UI verification.\n\n## Core Workflow\n\n1. Check whether the repo has a dedicated Odysseus screenshot or browser-navigation CLI command.\n- Prefer the dedicated `odysseus` command when it exists.\n- Check with `odysseus --help` or inspect `/Users/kevin/code/odysseus/src/odysseus/cli.py`.\n- If the dedicated command is absent, say so briefly in your own reasoning and use the Playwright fallback below.\n\n2. Reuse the Docker Compose app instead of starting a separate server.\n- Check the stack with `docker compose ps --format json`.\n- Treat `web` on `http://127.0.0.1:5173` and `api` on `http://127.0.0.1:8000` as the default local targets.\n- Never spin up a separate Vite or FastAPI server just for visual QA when the Compose stack is available.\n\n3. If the Compose stack is down during Codex code-editing work, start it automatically in a Codex terminal.\n- For Codex code-agent editing flows, do not stop to ask the user first.\n- Start the stack in detached mode with `docker compose up -d`.\n- Mention in an intermediary update that you started the stack because the visual QA workflow depends on the running app.\n- Only pause and surface the issue if the compose start fails.\n\n4. Capture a baseline screenshot before editing when current visuals matter.\n- Default to a desktop viewport of `1440x900`.\n- Use a second narrow/mobile viewport when the change could affect responsiveness.\n- Prefer viewport screenshots of the visible UI, not `fullPage`, unless the user specifically wants the entire scroll area.\n\n5. Navigate with stable selectors, then capture after edits.\n- Prefer role, label, and visible-text selectors over brittle structure selectors.\n- Wait for the relevant pane, menu, or state to settle before capture.\n- Compare before and after screenshots from the same route and viewport.\n\n## Current Fallback When The Dedicated CLI Is Absent\n\nCheck whether Compose is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose ps --format json\n```\n\nStart it automatically for Codex editing runs when needed:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose up -d\n```\n\nTake a straight viewport screenshot:\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kevin/code/odysseus/webui && npx playwright screenshot --viewport-size=1440,900 http://127.0.0.1:5173 /tmp/odysseus-visual-qa.png\n```\n\nFor scripted navigation, use a short task-specific Playwright script:\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kevin/code/odysseus/webui && node --input-type=module - <<'EOF'\nimport { chromium } from \"playwright\";\n\nconst browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });\nconst context = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1440, height: 900 } });\nconst page = await context.newPage();\n\nawait page.goto(\"http://127.0.0.1:5173\");\nawait page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"New Theme\" }).click();\nawait page.screenshot({ path: \"/tmp/odysseus-visual-qa.png\" });\n\nawait browser.close();\nEOF\n```\n\n## Guidelines\n\n- Use live screenshots to decide whether a styling tweak is actually complete.\n- Mention the exact screenshot path in the final response when you capture one.\n- Prefer `/tmp/odysseus-visual-qa-*.png` for throwaway artifacts unless the user asked for a project artifact.\n- For color, spacing, and hierarchy tweaks, compare before and after in the same viewport.\n- For larger UI restyling, verify at least one desktop viewport and one narrow/mobile viewport.\n- If the app fails to render or the screenshot workflow breaks, say exactly what blocked validation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:198:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_99","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /Users/kevin/code/storyteller/.codex/skills/webapp-qa/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: webapp-qa\ndescription: Use when interacting with a local webapp in this repo, taking screenshots, and verifying behavior both visually and functionally through the docker-compose dev stack and the bundled Puppeteer runner.\n---\n\n# Webapp QA\n\nUse this skill when the task involves running the local app, exercising it in a browser, taking screenshots, or checking that UI behavior and rendered output match expectations.\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. Start the local stack with `docker compose up -d --build`.\n2. Confirm the app is healthy with `docker compose ps`.\n3. Run browser checks from the `browser` service with `docker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec <spec-path>`.\n4. Review the generated screenshot under `.artifacts/webapp-qa/`.\n\n## App URLs\n\n- From the host machine, the app is available at `http://localhost:8566`.\n- From inside the browser container, use `http://frontend:8566`.\n\n## Puppeteer Specs\n\nThe reusable runner lives at `tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs`.\n\nSpecs are JSON files with this shape:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"url\": \"http://frontend:8566\",\n  \"outputPath\": \"/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa/example.png\",\n  \"viewport\": { \"width\": 1440, \"height\": 960 },\n  \"steps\": [\n    { \"action\": \"waitForSelector\", \"selector\": \"[data-testid='app-card']\" },\n    { \"action\": \"assertText\", \"text\": \"Hello from FastAPI!\" },\n    { \"action\": \"screenshot\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nSupported actions:\n\n- `goto`\n- `waitForSelector`\n- `waitForText`\n- `click`\n- `type`\n- `press`\n- `hover`\n- `assertSelector`\n- `assertText`\n- `assertUrlIncludes`\n- `waitForTimeout`\n- `screenshot`\n\n## Example\n\nUse `tools/webapp-qa/examples/homepage.spec.json` for a basic smoke test plus screenshot:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec ./examples/homepage.spec.json\n```\n\n## Verification Guidance\n\n- For functional checks, prefer explicit `assertText`, `assertSelector`, and URL assertions over informal observation.\n- For visual checks, capture a screenshot after the page reaches the expected stable state.\n- If a page depends on backend data, verify the user-visible result instead of only checking network readiness.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:202:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_101","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'cat tools/webapp-qa/package.json'","aggregated_output":"{\n  \"name\": \"storyteller-webapp-qa\",\n  \"private\": true,\n  \"version\": \"1.0.0\",\n  \"type\": \"module\",\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"check\": \"node ./scripts/run-spec.mjs\",\n    \"check:example\": \"node ./scripts/run-spec.mjs --spec ./examples/homepage.spec.json\"\n  },\n  \"dependencies\": {\n    \"puppeteer\": \"^24.0.0\"\n  }\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:204:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_102","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml\"","aggregated_output":"name: storyteller\n\nservices:\n  postgres:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    restart: unless-stopped\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_DB: storyteller\n      POSTGRES_USER: storyteller\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: storyteller\n    ports:\n      - \"8567:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD-SHELL\n        - pg_isready -U storyteller -d storyteller\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  gcs:\n    image: fsouza/fake-gcs-server:1.54.0\n    restart: unless-stopped\n    command:\n      - -scheme\n      - http\n      - -backend\n      - filesystem\n      - -filesystem-root\n      - /data\n      - -public-host\n      - localhost:8568\n      - -external-url\n      - http://localhost:8568\n    ports:\n      - \"8568:4443\"\n    volumes:\n      - gcs_data:/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD-SHELL\n        - wget -q -O - http://127.0.0.1:4443/storage/v1/b >/dev/null\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  backend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../backend\n    depends_on:\n      postgres:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      gcs:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: /workspace/secrets.yaml\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    ports:\n      - \"8565:8565\"\n    command: python -m app\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - python\n        - -c\n        - |\n          import sys, urllib.request\n          urllib.request.urlopen(\"http://127.0.0.1:8565/health\")\n          sys.exit(0)\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  worker:\n    build:\n      context: ../../backend\n    depends_on:\n      postgres:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      gcs:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    restart: unless-stopped\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: \"\"\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY: ${STORYTELLER_GEMINI_API_KEY:-worker-placeholder-key}\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    command:\n      - python\n      - -m\n      - app.worker\n      - --poll-interval-seconds\n      - \"2\"\n      - --lease-duration-seconds\n      - \"60\"\n\n  frontend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../frontend\n    depends_on:\n      backend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    environment:\n      CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: \"true\"\n      VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565\n    volumes:\n      - ../../frontend:/app\n      - frontend_node_modules:/app/node_modules\n    ports:\n      - \"8566:8566\"\n    command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8566\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - node\n        - -e\n        - |\n          require(\"http\").get(\"http://127.0.0.1:8566\", (response) => {\n            process.exit(response.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1);\n          }).on(\"error\", () => process.exit(1));\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 10s\n\n  browser:\n    build:\n      context: ../../tools/webapp-qa\n    depends_on:\n      frontend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa\n    environment:\n      PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium\n      TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566\n    volumes:\n      - ../../:/workspace\n      - webapp_qa_node_modules:/workspace/tools/webapp-qa/node_modules\n    command: sleep infinity\n\nvolumes:\n  frontend_node_modules:\n  postgres_data:\n  gcs_data:\n  webapp_qa_node_modules:\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:208:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_104","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs\"","aggregated_output":"import fs from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport path from \"node:path\";\nimport process from \"node:process\";\n\nimport puppeteer from \"puppeteer\";\n\nfunction parseArgs(argv) {\n  const args = {};\n\n  for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {\n    const token = argv[index];\n\n    if (!token.startsWith(\"--\")) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const key = token.slice(2);\n    const value = argv[index + 1];\n    args[key] = value;\n    index += 1;\n  }\n\n  return args;\n}\n\nasync function loadSpec(specPath) {\n  const resolvedPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), specPath);\n  const rawSpec = await fs.readFile(resolvedPath, \"utf8\");\n  const spec = JSON.parse(rawSpec);\n  return {\n    resolvedPath,\n    spec,\n  };\n}\n\nasync function ensureParentDirectory(filePath) {\n  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });\n}\n\nasync function saveFailureScreenshot(\n  page,\n  outputPath = \"../../.artifacts/webapp-qa/failure.png\",\n) {\n  const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n  await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n  await page.screenshot({\n    path: resolvedOutputPath,\n    fullPage: true,\n  });\n  console.error(`Saved failure screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n}\n\nasync function waitForText(page, text, timeout) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    (expectedText) => document.body?.innerText.includes(expectedText),\n    { timeout },\n    text,\n  );\n}\n\nasync function assertText(page, text, timeout) {\n  try {\n    await waitForText(page, text, timeout);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body?.innerText || \"\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `Expected text not found: \"${text}\"\\nCurrent page text:\\n${bodyText}`,\n      { cause: error },\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runStep(page, step, defaultTimeout, fallbackOutputPath) {\n  const timeout = step.timeout ?? defaultTimeout;\n\n  switch (step.action) {\n    case \"goto\":\n      await page.goto(step.url, {\n        waitUntil: step.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForText\":\n      await waitForText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"click\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"type\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector, { clickCount: 3 });\n      if (step.clear ?? true) {\n        await page.keyboard.press(\"Backspace\");\n      }\n      await page.type(step.selector, step.text, {\n        delay: step.delay ?? 20,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"press\":\n      await page.keyboard.press(step.key);\n      return;\n\n    case \"hover\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.hover(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertText\":\n      await assertText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertUrlIncludes\":\n      await page.waitForFunction(\n        (expectedFragment) => window.location.href.includes(expectedFragment),\n        { timeout },\n        step.value,\n      );\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForTimeout\":\n      await new Promise((resolve) => {\n        setTimeout(resolve, step.timeout ?? 500);\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"screenshot\": {\n      const outputPath = path.resolve(\n        process.cwd(),\n        step.outputPath ?? fallbackOutputPath,\n      );\n      await ensureParentDirectory(outputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: outputPath,\n        fullPage: step.fullPage ?? true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${outputPath}`);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    default:\n      throw new Error(`Unsupported step action: ${step.action}`);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));\n\n  if (!args.spec) {\n    throw new Error(\"Usage: npm run check -- --spec <path-to-spec.json>\");\n  }\n\n  const { resolvedPath, spec } = await loadSpec(args.spec);\n  const launchOptions = {\n    args: [\"--no-sandbox\", \"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"],\n    headless: spec.headless ?? true,\n  };\n\n  if (process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH) {\n    launchOptions.executablePath = process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH;\n  }\n\n  if (spec.launchArgs?.length) {\n    launchOptions.args = [...launchOptions.args, ...spec.launchArgs];\n  }\n\n  const browser = await puppeteer.launch(launchOptions);\n\n  try {\n    const page = await browser.newPage();\n    const viewport = spec.viewport ?? { width: 1440, height: 960 };\n    const timeout = spec.timeout ?? 15000;\n    const outputPath = spec.outputPath ?? \"./.artifacts/webapp-qa/latest.png\";\n\n    await page.setViewport(viewport);\n    page.setDefaultTimeout(timeout);\n    page.on(\"console\", (message) => {\n      console.log(`[browser:${message.type()}] ${message.text()}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"pageerror\", (error) => {\n      console.error(`[browser:pageerror] ${error.message}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"requestfailed\", (request) => {\n      console.error(\n        `[browser:requestfailed] ${request.method()} ${request.url()} ${request.failure()?.errorText || \"\"}`,\n      );\n    });\n\n    if (spec.url) {\n      await page.goto(spec.url, {\n        waitUntil: spec.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n    }\n\n    for (const step of spec.steps ?? []) {\n      await runStep(page, step, timeout, outputPath);\n    }\n\n    if (!spec.steps?.some((step) => step.action === \"screenshot\")) {\n      const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n      await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: resolvedOutputPath,\n        fullPage: true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n    }\n\n    console.log(`Completed spec ${resolvedPath}`);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const pages = await browser.pages();\n    const page = pages.at(-1);\n\n    if (page) {\n      await saveFailureScreenshot(page);\n    }\n\n    throw error;\n  } finally {\n    await browser.close();\n  }\n}\n\nmain().catch((error) => {\n  console.error(error instanceof Error ? error.stack : error);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

    ./prompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.codex.jsonlines:302:{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_158","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -c 'LATEST_SESSION_ID=$(curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8565/api/v1/sessions | jq -r '\"'.[0].id'); docker compose -f infra/compose/docker-compose.yml run --rm --no-deps browser node --input-type=module <<'EOF'\nimport fs from 'node:fs/promises';\nimport puppeteer from 'puppeteer';\n\nconst sessionId = process.env.LATEST_SESSION_ID;\nconst baseUrl = 'http://192.168.86.47:4273/sessions/' + sessionId;\nconst outputPath = '/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa/prompt-21-new-session-workspace.png';\n\nawait fs.mkdir('/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa', { recursive: true });\n\nconst browser = await puppeteer.launch({\n  executablePath: process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH,\n  headless: true,\n  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],\n});\n\ntry {\n  const page = await browser.newPage();\n  await page.setViewport({ width: 1440, height: 960 });\n  await page.goto(baseUrl, { waitUntil: 'networkidle2', timeout: 15000 });\n  await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid=\\\"workspace-route\\\"]', {\n    visible: true,\n    timeout: 15000,\n  });\n  await page.screenshot({ path: outputPath, fullPage: true });\n  console.log('Saved screenshot to ' + outputPath);\n} finally {\n  await browser.close();\n}\nEOF\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}

../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: /workspace/secrets.yaml\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    ports:\n      - \"8565:8565\"\n    command: python -m app\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - python\n        - -c\n        - |\n          import sys, urllib.request\n          urllib.request.urlopen(\"http://127.0.0.1:8565/health\")\n          sys.exit(0)\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  frontend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../frontend\n    depends_on:\n      backend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    environment:\n      CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: \"true\"\n      VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n      - ../../:/workspace:ro\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n      STORYTELLER_SECRETS_FILE: /workspace/secrets.yaml\n      STORYTELLER_ENVIRONMENT: development\n      STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL: postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT: http://gcs:4443\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID: storyteller-local\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL: http://localhost:8568\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_SESSIONS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-sessions\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_AUDIO_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-audio\n      STORYTELLER_GCS_EXPORTS_BUCKET_NAME: storyteller-exports\n    ports:\n      - \"8565:8565\"\n    command: python -m app\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - python\n        - -c\n        - |\n          import sys, urllib.request\n          urllib.request.urlopen(\"http://127.0.0.1:8565/health\")\n          sys.exit(0)\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  frontend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../frontend\n    depends_on:\n      backend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    environment:\n      CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: \"true\"\n      VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565\n    volumes:\n      - ../../frontend:/app\n      - frontend_node_modules:/app/node_modules\n    ports:\n      - \"8566:8566\"\n    command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8566\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - node\n        - -e\n        - |\n          require(\"http\").get(\"http://127.0.0.1:8566\", (response) => {\n            process.exit(response.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1);\n          }).on(\"error\", () => process.exit(1));\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 10s\n\n  browser:\n    build:\n      context: ../../tools/webapp-qa\n    depends_on:\n      frontend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa\n    environment:\n      PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium\n      TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566\n    volumes:\n      - ../../:/workspace\n      - webapp_qa_node_modules:/workspace/tools/webapp-qa/node_modules\n    command: sleep infinity\n\nvolumes:\n  frontend_node_modules:\n  postgres_data:\n  gcs_data:\n  webapp_qa_node_modules:\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_8", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 21, "prompt_index": 14, "prompt_name": "13 \u2014 Storage Abstraction and Bucket Strategy", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T23:06:42-07:00"}

    ./prompts/23-frontend-state-foundation.codex.jsonlines:51:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_25","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' /Users/kevin/code/storyteller/.codex/skills/webapp-qa/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: webapp-qa\ndescription: Use when interacting with a local webapp in this repo, taking screenshots, and verifying behavior both visually and functionally through the docker-compose dev stack and the bundled Puppeteer runner.\n---\n\n# Webapp QA\n\nUse this skill when the task involves running the local app, exercising it in a browser, taking screenshots, or checking that UI behavior and rendered output match expectations.\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. Start the local stack with `docker compose up -d --build`.\n2. Confirm the app is healthy with `docker compose ps`.\n3. Run browser checks from the `browser` service with `docker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec <spec-path>`.\n4. Review the generated screenshot under `.artifacts/webapp-qa/`.\n\n## App URLs\n\n- From the host machine, the app is available at `http://localhost:8566`.\n- From inside the browser container, use `http://frontend:8566`.\n\n## Puppeteer Specs\n\nThe reusable runner lives at `tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs`.\n\nSpecs are JSON files with this shape:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"url\": \"http://frontend:8566\",\n  \"outputPath\": \"/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa/example.png\",\n  \"viewport\": { \"width\": 1440, \"height\": 960 },\n  \"steps\": [\n    { \"action\": \"waitForSelector\", \"selector\": \"[data-testid='app-card']\" },\n    { \"action\": \"assertText\", \"text\": \"Hello from FastAPI!\" },\n    { \"action\": \"screenshot\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nSupported actions:\n\n- `goto`\n- `waitForSelector`\n- `waitForText`\n- `click`\n- `type`\n- `press`\n- `hover`\n- `assertSelector`\n- `assertText`\n- `assertUrlIncludes`\n- `waitForTimeout`\n- `screenshot`\n\n## Example\n\nUse `tools/webapp-qa/examples/homepage.spec.json` for a basic smoke test plus screenshot:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec ./examples/homepage.spec.json\n```\n\n## Verification Guidance\n\n- For functional checks, prefer explicit `assertText`, `assertSelector`, and URL assertions over informal observation.\n- For visual checks, capture a screenshot after the page reaches the expected stable state.\n- If a page depends on backend data, verify the user-visible result instead of only checking network readiness.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs\"","aggregated_output":"import fs from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport path from \"node:path\";\nimport process from \"node:process\";\n\nimport puppeteer from \"puppeteer\";\n\nfunction parseArgs(argv) {\n  const args = {};\n\n  for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {\n    const token = argv[index];\n\n    if (!token.startsWith(\"--\")) {\n      continue;\n    }\n\n    const key = token.slice(2);\n    const value = argv[index + 1];\n    args[key] = value;\n    index += 1;\n  }\n\n  return args;\n}\n\nasync function loadSpec(specPath) {\n  const resolvedPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), specPath);\n  const rawSpec = await fs.readFile(resolvedPath, \"utf8\");\n  const spec = JSON.parse(rawSpec);\n  return {\n    resolvedPath,\n    spec,\n  };\n}\n\nasync function ensureParentDirectory(filePath) {\n  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });\n}\n\nasync function saveFailureScreenshot(\n  page,\n  outputPath = \"../../.artifacts/webapp-qa/failure.png\",\n) {\n  const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n  await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n  await page.screenshot({\n    path: resolvedOutputPath,\n    fullPage: true,\n  });\n  console.error(`Saved failure screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n}\n\nasync function waitForText(page, text, timeout) {\n  await page.waitForFunction(\n    (expectedText) => document.body?.innerText.includes(expectedText),\n    { timeout },\n    text,\n  );\n}\n\nasync function assertText(page, text, timeout) {\n  try {\n    await waitForText(page, text, timeout);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const bodyText = await page.evaluate(() => document.body?.innerText || \"\");\n    throw new Error(\n      `Expected text not found: \"${text}\"\\nCurrent page text:\\n${bodyText}`,\n      { cause: error },\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runStep(page, step, defaultTimeout, fallbackOutputPath) {\n  const timeout = step.timeout ?? defaultTimeout;\n\n  switch (step.action) {\n    case \"goto\":\n      await page.goto(step.url, {\n        waitUntil: step.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForText\":\n      await waitForText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"click\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"type\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.click(step.selector, { clickCount: 3 });\n      if (step.clear ?? true) {\n        await page.keyboard.press(\"Backspace\");\n      }\n      await page.type(step.selector, step.text, {\n        delay: step.delay ?? 20,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"press\":\n      await page.keyboard.press(step.key);\n      return;\n\n    case \"hover\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: true,\n      });\n      await page.hover(step.selector);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertSelector\":\n      await page.waitForSelector(step.selector, {\n        timeout,\n        visible: step.visible ?? true,\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertText\":\n      await assertText(page, step.text, timeout);\n      return;\n\n    case \"assertUrlIncludes\":\n      await page.waitForFunction(\n        (expectedFragment) => window.location.href.includes(expectedFragment),\n        { timeout },\n        step.value,\n      );\n      return;\n\n    case \"waitForTimeout\":\n      await new Promise((resolve) => {\n        setTimeout(resolve, step.timeout ?? 500);\n      });\n      return;\n\n    case \"screenshot\": {\n      const outputPath = path.resolve(\n        process.cwd(),\n        step.outputPath ?? fallbackOutputPath,\n      );\n      await ensureParentDirectory(outputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: outputPath,\n        fullPage: step.fullPage ?? true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${outputPath}`);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    default:\n      throw new Error(`Unsupported step action: ${step.action}`);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));\n\n  if (!args.spec) {\n    throw new Error(\"Usage: npm run check -- --spec <path-to-spec.json>\");\n  }\n\n  const { resolvedPath, spec } = await loadSpec(args.spec);\n  const launchOptions = {\n    args: [\"--no-sandbox\", \"--disable-setuid-sandbox\"],\n    headless: spec.headless ?? true,\n  };\n\n  if (process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH) {\n    launchOptions.executablePath = process.env.PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH;\n  }\n\n  if (spec.launchArgs?.length) {\n    launchOptions.args = [...launchOptions.args, ...spec.launchArgs];\n  }\n\n  const browser = await puppeteer.launch(launchOptions);\n\n  try {\n    const page = await browser.newPage();\n    const viewport = spec.viewport ?? { width: 1440, height: 960 };\n    const timeout = spec.timeout ?? 15000;\n    const outputPath = spec.outputPath ?? \"./.artifacts/webapp-qa/latest.png\";\n\n    await page.setViewport(viewport);\n    page.setDefaultTimeout(timeout);\n    page.on(\"console\", (message) => {\n      console.log(`[browser:${message.type()}] ${message.text()}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"pageerror\", (error) => {\n      console.error(`[browser:pageerror] ${error.message}`);\n    });\n    page.on(\"requestfailed\", (request) => {\n      console.error(\n        `[browser:requestfailed] ${request.method()} ${request.url()} ${request.failure()?.errorText || \"\"}`,\n      );\n    });\n\n    if (spec.url) {\n      await page.goto(spec.url, {\n        waitUntil: spec.waitUntil ?? \"networkidle2\",\n        timeout,\n      });\n    }\n\n    for (const step of spec.steps ?? []) {\n      await runStep(page, step, timeout, outputPath);\n    }\n\n    if (!spec.steps?.some((step) => step.action === \"screenshot\")) {\n      const resolvedOutputPath = path.resolve(process.cwd(), outputPath);\n      await ensureParentDirectory(resolvedOutputPath);\n      await page.screenshot({\n        path: resolvedOutputPath,\n        fullPage: true,\n      });\n      console.log(`Saved screenshot to ${resolvedOutputPath}`);\n    }\n\n    console.log(`Completed spec ${resolvedPath}`);\n  } catch (error) {\n    const pages = await browser.pages();\n    const page = pages.at(-1);\n\n    if (page) {\n      await saveFailureScreenshot(page);\n    }\n\n    throw error;\n  } finally {\n    await browser.close();\n  }\n}\n\nmain().catch((error) => {\n  console.error(error instanceof Error ? error.stack : error);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
    ./prompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.codex.jsonlines:28:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml\"","aggregated_output":"services:\n  backend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../backend\n    working_dir: /app\n    volumes:\n      - ../../backend:/app\n    environment:\n      PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: \"1\"\n      PYTHONUNBUFFERED: \"1\"\n    ports:\n      - \"8565:8565\"\n    command: uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8565\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - python\n        - -c\n        - |\n          import sys, urllib.request\n          urllib.request.urlopen(\"http://127.0.0.1:8565/api/hello\")\n          sys.exit(0)\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 5s\n\n  frontend:\n    build:\n      context: ../../frontend\n    depends_on:\n      backend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /app\n    environment:\n      CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING: \"true\"\n      VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET: http://backend:8565\n    volumes:\n      - ../../frontend:/app\n      - /app/node_modules\n    ports:\n      - \"8566:8566\"\n    command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8566\n    healthcheck:\n      test:\n        - CMD\n        - node\n        - -e\n        - |\n          require(\"http\").get(\"http://127.0.0.1:8566\", (response) => {\n            process.exit(response.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1);\n          }).on(\"error\", () => process.exit(1));\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 10s\n\n  browser:\n    build:\n      context: ../../tools/webapp-qa\n    depends_on:\n      frontend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa\n    environment:\n      PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium\n      TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566\n    volumes:\n      - ../../:/workspace\n      - webapp_qa_node_modules:/workspace/tools/webapp-qa/node_modules\n    command: sleep infinity\n\nvolumes:\n  webapp_qa_node_modules:\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

0 : 1);\n          }).on(\"error\", () => process.exit(1));\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 20\n      start_period: 10s\n\n  browser:\n    build:\n      context: ../../tools/webapp-qa\n    depends_on:\n      frontend:\n        condition: service_healthy\n    working_dir: /workspace/tools/webapp-qa\n    environment:\n      PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH: /usr/bin/chromium\n      TARGET_BASE_URL: http://frontend:8566\n    volumes:\n      - ../../:/workspace\n      - webapp_qa_node_modules:/workspace/tools/webapp-qa/node_modules\n    command: sleep infinity\n\nvolumes:\n  webapp_qa_node_modules:\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' infra/compose/docker-compose.yml\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_17", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 38, "prompt_index": 3, "prompt_name": "02 \u2014 Scaffold the React Frontend with Vite", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T21:08:09-07:00"}
    ./prompts/02-vite-react-scaffold.yolopilot.jsonlines:41:{"color": "cyan", "emoji": "\ud83d\udee0\ufe0f", "event_type": "Command Finished", "message": "completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' /Users/kevin/code/storyteller/.codex/skills/webapp-qa/SKILL.md\"", "raw_event": {"item": {"aggregated_output": "---\nname: webapp-qa\ndescription: Use when interacting with a local webapp in this repo, taking screenshots, and verifying behavior both visually and functionally through the docker-compose dev stack and the bundled Puppeteer runner.\n---\n\n# Webapp QA\n\nUse this skill when the task involves running the local app, exercising it in a browser, taking screenshots, or checking that UI behavior and rendered output match expectations.\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. Start the local stack with `docker compose up -d --build`.\n2. Confirm the app is healthy with `docker compose ps`.\n3. Run browser checks from the `browser` service with `docker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec <spec-path>`.\n4. Review the generated screenshot under `.artifacts/webapp-qa/`.\n\n## App URLs\n\n- From the host machine, the app is available at `http://localhost:8566`.\n- From inside the browser container, use `http://frontend:8566`.\n\n## Puppeteer Specs\n\nThe reusable runner lives at `tools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs`.\n\nSpecs are JSON files with this shape:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"url\": \"http://frontend:8566\",\n  \"outputPath\": \"/workspace/.artifacts/webapp-qa/example.png\",\n  \"viewport\": { \"width\": 1440, \"height\": 960 },\n  \"steps\": [\n    { \"action\": \"waitForSelector\", \"selector\": \"[data-testid='app-card']\" },\n    { \"action\": \"assertText\", \"text\": \"Hello from FastAPI!\" },\n    { \"action\": \"screenshot\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nSupported actions:\n\n- `goto`\n- `waitForSelector`\n- `waitForText`\n- `click`\n- `type`\n- `press`\n- `hover`\n- `assertSelector`\n- `assertText`\n- `assertUrlIncludes`\n- `waitForTimeout`\n- `screenshot`\n\n## Example\n\nUse `tools/webapp-qa/examples/homepage.spec.json` for a basic smoke test plus screenshot:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose run --rm browser npm run check -- --spec ./examples/homepage.spec.json\n```\n\n## Verification Guidance\n\n- For functional checks, prefer explicit `assertText`, `assertSelector`, and URL assertions over informal observation.\n- For visual checks, capture a screenshot after the page reaches the expected stable state.\n- If a page depends on backend data, verify the user-visible result instead of only checking network readiness.\n", "command": "/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,240p' /Users/kevin/code/storyteller/.codex/skills/webapp-qa/SKILL.md\"", "exit_code": 0, "id": "item_19", "status": "completed", "type": "command_execution"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 41, "prompt_index": 3, "prompt_name": "02 \u2014 Scaffold the React Frontend with Vite", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T21:08:19-07:00"}

Before editing, I\u2019m checking whether the curr...", "raw_event": {"item": {"id": "item_22", "text": "I\u2019m using the `webapp-qa` skill for verification later because this change introduces a new browser surface and the repo already has a Puppeteer-based smoke path wired through Docker Compose. Before editing, I\u2019m checking whether the current QA example assumes the old \u201cHello, world!\u201d placeholder so I can update it alongside the app.", "type": "agent_message"}, "type": "item.completed"}, "task_display_context": {"event_number": 46, "prompt_index": 3, "prompt_name": "02 \u2014 Scaffold the React Frontend with Vite", "prompt_total": 100}, "timestap": "2026-03-31T21:08:26-07:00"}

oper-debug-inspector.md\\nprompts/25-chat-window-foundation.md\\nprompts/60-composition-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/96-compose-dev-and-prod-shapes.md\\nprompts/49-beat-sheet-refinement-ui-and-chat.md\\nprompts/93-accessibility-pass-and-keyboard-support.md\\nprompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\\nprompts/47-character-refinement-via-chat.md\\nprompts/16-asset-metadata-and-file-records.md\\nprompts/76-audio-preview-and-segment-status-ui.md\\nprompts/53-outline-drill-down.md\\nprompts/66-rewrite-prior-segments.md\\nprompts/18-postgres-job-runner-skeleton.md\\nprompts/43-brief-normalization-service.md\\nprompts/base_prompt.md\\nprompts/50-story-length-duration-and-chapter-setup.md\\nprompts/86-completed-session-summary-and-search.md\\nprompts/82-html-markdown-and-reader-formatting.md\\nprompts/77-final-audio-assembly.md\\nprompts/79-audio-pipeline-tests.md\\nprompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\\nprompts/89-full-journey-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/57-composition-prompt-assembly.md\\nprompts/39-bridge-and-replay-tests.md\\nprompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.md\\nprompts/31-intent-parser-service.md\\nprompts/75-audio-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md\\nprompts/85-audio-player-with-text-sync-hooks.md\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.md\\nprompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\\nprompts/08-repo-quality-tooling.md\\nprompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.md\\nprompts/73-gemini-tts-integration.md\\nprompts/71-audio-length-estimation.md\\nprompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.md\\nprompts/38-session-replay-and-resume-hydration.md\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.md\\nprompts/40-genre-selection-api-and-ui.md\\nprompts/70-audio-settings-model-and-ui.md\\nprompts/69-composition-loop-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines\\nprompts/27-reusable-form-and-card-components.md\\nprompts/98-demo-data-and-onboarding.md\\nprompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.md\\nprompts/51-correlation-heuristics.md\\nprompts/35-chat-commands-and-quick-actions.md\\nprompts/55-continuity-bible.md\\nprompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.md\\nprompts/32-ui-action-policy-engine.md\\nprompts/94-performance-pass.md\\nprompts/29-frontend-websocket-client-skeleton.md\\nprompts/58-usage-cost-and-latency-metrics.md\\nprompts/68-autosave-drafts-and-partials.md\\nprompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.md\\nprompts/91-rate-limits-retries-and-fallbacks.md\\nprompts/24-design-tokens-and-accessibility-foundation.md\\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:13:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_6\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/00-project-charter.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# 00 — Project Charter and Success Criteria\\n\\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nCreate the initial project framing for a full-stack app that helps a user build a bedtime story, turn it into audio, and resume past sessions.\\n\\n## Build\\n- Write a top-level README that explains the product vision, the end-to-end story workflow, the required stages, and the major technical choices already fixed by the brief.\\n- Document the non-negotiable requirements: React + Vite frontend, Python FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL for all structured data, a file-backed GCS emulator for blobs, Docker Compose for local orchestration, and `secrets.yaml` kept out of git.\\n- Add a short architecture note describing why all Gemini calls must happen on the backend and why long-running composition and audio jobs need resumable server-side state.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n- `README.md` with product overview and local development expectations\\n- `docs/product-brief.md` or equivalent design note\\n- `docs/architecture-overview.md` with the first cut of the system picture\\n\\n## Acceptance checks\\n\\n- A new engineer can read the repo root and understand what is being built.\\n- The required story stages from the brief are explicitly listed in order.\\n- The first screen requirement — a past-sessions screen for resume/edit/create — is called out early, not buried.\\n\\n## Notes\\n\\nDo not generate implementation code yet beyond lightweight documentation scaffolding.\\n\\n## Suggested commit label\\n\\n`feat(prompt-00): project charter`\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:14:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_5\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# Base Prompt — Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` — React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` — FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` — Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` — architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- “Selected genre: Quest Fantasy”\\n- “Updated target runtime: ~12 minutes”\\n- “Accepted revised beat sheet”\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n./prompts/00-project-charter.codex.jsonlines:18:{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_9\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p'
